reason: Moses, the Hebrew leader, demonstrates clear accountability for his people's welfare throughout this passage. He repeatedly confronts Pharaoh on God's behalf demanding the Israelites' release (appearing at least ten times with the message 'שַׁלַּח אֶת־ עַמִּי' - 'Let my people go'). He intercedes with God on the people's behalf regarding the plagues (וַיִּצְעַ֤ק מֹשֶׁה֙ אֶל־ יְהוָה - 'Moses cried unto the LORD'). When negotiating their departure, Moses insists their entire welfare be considered: young and old, sons and daughters, flocks and herds. Crucially, Moses receives and communicates God's Passover instructions directly to the elders, taking responsibility to ensure the people know how to secure their salvation. He thus demonstrates leadership accountability through advocacy, intercession, protective guidance, and faithful transmission of life-preserving instructions.
